Document:
# Alert Level Procedures Intro This handles general non-threat specific proceedures [bold][color=#004d75]Security[/bold][/color] should be following on any given Alert Level, unless stated otherwise in the [bold][textlink="Security Almanac" link="security-almanac"][/bold]
## Code Green [color=green]"Station nominal. Nothing is wrong."[/color]
- Any member of [bold][color=#004d75]Security[/bold][/color], with the exception of [bold][color=#004d75]Cadets[/bold][/color] may hold at most one lethal sidearm, and two less-than-lethal sidearms at any time.
Less-than-lethal grenades, such as stingers, flashbangs or teargas/smoke grenades are also allowed, lethal grenades such as blast/fragmentation grenades or EMPs are not.
[bold][color=#004d75]Cadets[/bold][/color] are only permitted two Less-Than-Lethal firearms.
Hybrid firearms, such as the Dominator can either count towards the Lethal Sidearm or Less-Than-Lethal Sidearm count, not both. It depends on if the member of [bold][color=#004d75]Security Staff[/bold][/color] intends to use it for lethal purposes or not.
Intent to use for lethal purposes is defined as carrying item(s) that either add lethal capabilities to an otherwise less-than-lethal sidearm, such as carrying lethal rounds on your person for your sidearm or carrying multiple hybrid firearms.
Eg. Carrying an MK 78 with both Rubber and Lethal bullets on your person counts as a Lethal Sidearm. If it is only rubber, then it is counted as a Less-Than-Lethal Sidearm.
The Security Flaregun, as it is provided in your Survival Kit, does not count towards this, but you may only carry one on you at all times.
- Longarms are not to be taken outside of [bold][color=#004d75]Security[/bold][/color], with the exception of the [bold][color=#00527d]Warden[/bold][/color] and [bold][color=#DB9D3B]Head of Security[/bold][/color], which may carry one longarm of their choice, within reason.
In other words, the [bold][color=#DB9D3B]Head of Security[/bold][/color] & [bold][color=#00527d]Warden[/bold][/color] cannot take the M492 or other similar weapons out of the armory as their longarm.
A longarm is defined as any firearm that cannot fit in your belt holsters or if not applicable, your pocket, has a long barrel or is otherwise cumbersome to carry.
- Random Searches are not allowed.
Searches with cause, defined as; - An individual performing criminal activity in front of a member of [bold][color=#004d75]Security Staff[/bold][/color] - An individual’s crime being witnessed and reported by a member of [bold][color=#004d75]Security[/bold][/color] or [bold][color=#e6b363]Command Staff[/bold][/color]
May be performed without a warrant. Any other searches, such as looking for evidence of a crime, requires a warrant.
## Code Yellow [color=yellow]"There is a localized structural or atmospheric threat within the station."[/color]
-[bold][color=#004d75]Security Staff[/bold][/color] are required to work together with Engineering Staff to safely cordon off the affected area(s) and prevent any entry that would put crewmembers at risk.
This is waived if there is a [bold][color=red]Threat[/bold][/color] on the station.
Crewmembers attempting to get into the area(s) that have caused the [bold][color=yellow]Code Yellow[/color][/bold] alert level may be charged with Refusal to Comply & Endangerment.
## Code Blue [color=#1E90FF]"There is a minor threat onboard or in the station’s nearby space that may harm crew."[/color]
[bold]Disarmament grace period for Alert Level shifting to [color=green]Green[/color] is 5 minutes.[/bold]
-Longarm permits, additional sidearm permits and lethal grenade permits may be issued to or requested by [bold][color=#004d75]Security Staff[/bold][/color], with the exception of [bold][color=#004d75]Cadets[/bold][/color].
-[bold][color=#004d75]Cadets[/bold][/color] are only allowed one lethal sidearm, and may request one from the Armory without a permit.
-Armor can be requested by [bold][color=#004d75]Security Staff[/bold][/color] without a permit or be handed out at the [bold][color=#00527d]Warden[/bold][/color]’s discretion.
- Pairing up with other members of [bold][color=#004d75]Security Staff[/bold][/color] is recommended, depending on the exact nature of the threat.
-Random Searches are both permitted and encouraged. Refusing a random search or fleeing from one is grounds for Refusal to Comply.
This also applies to [bold][color=red]Code Red[/bold][/color] and higher.
Searches of entire departments still require a warrant, due to hampering productivity greatly.
## Code Red [bold][color=red]"There is a major threat onboard or in the station’s nearby space that threatens the entire crew."[/bold][/color]
[bold]Disarmament grace period for alert level shifting to [color=#1E90FF]Blue[/color]/[color=green]Green[/color] is 10 minutes[/bold]
- [bold][color=#004d75]Security Staff[/bold][/color] are given the authority to forcefully relocate members of the crew to their respective departments or other locations that are deemed “safe” from the current threat.
This also applies to other alert levels, such as [bold][color=violet]Code Violet[/bold][/color] & [bold][color=pink]Code Gamma[/bold][/color].
-Longarms, Armor & Lethal Grenades are allowed to be utilized without a permit, and are recommended to be distributed to [bold][color=#004d75]Security Staff[/bold][/color] by the [bold][color=#00527d]Warden[/bold][/color] or [bold][color=#DB9D3B]Head of Security[/bold][/color]. -Departmental Searches without a warrant may be performed. -Usage of M492 may or may not be permitted, refer to the [bold][textlink="Security Almanac" link="security-almanac"][/bold] to see if it is.
## Code Violet [bold][color=violet]"There is a viral threat onboard the station, Medical Staff are advised to isolate crewmembers with any symptoms."[/bold][/color]
-[bold][color=#004d75]Security Staff[/bold][/color] are required to work together with Medical Staff to enforce quarantine of infected individuals untill Medical can asertain the nature of the outbreak. If the disease is found to be not life-threatening, [bold][color=#004d75]Security[/bold][/color] may return to normal duties.
This is waived if there is a [bold][color=red]Threat[/bold][/color] on the station.
# Special Codes These codes can only be called/triggered under very specific circumstances, and are usually the result of extremely dire situations.
## Code Gamma [bold][color=pink]"There is a threat that poses an imminent risk of station-destruction and the death of every employee onboard."[/bold][/color]
-[bold][color=#004d75]Security Staff[/bold][/color] are given the authority to take any action deemed necessary to save the station, and any disruptive activity towards that goal will lead to immediate and indefinite imprisonment until the threat is eliminated.
-If [bold][color=pink]Emergency Response Teams[/bold][/color] are on-site, [bold][color=#004d75]Security[/bold][/color] must coordinate with the Team(s) to ensure station safety.
-Any form of attack on [bold][color=#004d75]Security[/bold][/color] & [bold][color=#e6b363]Command Staff[/bold][/color] or [bold][color=pink]Emergency Response Teams[/bold][/color] can be met with lethal force.
-Typically contraband-restricted equipment, such as [bold][color=darkred]Syndicate/Soviet Equipment/Weapons[/bold][/color] is permitted for emergency use.
This also applies on [bold][color=darkred]Code Delta.[/bold][/color]
## Code Delta [bold][color=darkred]"The on-board Nuclear Fission Device has been activated. If this was an accident, there is still time to defuse it."[/bold][/color]
-[bold][color=#004d75]Security Staff[/bold][/color] are given the ultimate authority to take any action deemed necessary to prevent or enforce the detonation of the on-board nuclear device, and any disruptive activity towards that goal will lead to [bold][color=red]immediate field-execution.[/bold][/color]
-[bold][color=#004d75]Security Staff[/bold][/color] must focus all attention on defusing the on-board nuclear device at all costs, unless it was an intentional activation by [bold][color=#e6b363]Command Staff[/bold][/color]. Retreating, fleeing, or otherwise leaving the station without disarming the on-board nuclear device will result in termination of your contract when you arrive at [bold][color=#1E4F19]Central Command[/bold][/color].
This is waived if the automated “Get to the pods” announcement occurs, as it will be too late to disarm the nuke.
